name: menubar
title: Menubar
description: A horizontal application menu bar (File / Edit / View) built on Base UI's Menubar, which coordinates a row of Base UI Menus under one role=menubar. Left/Right Arrow rove between top menus with a roving tabindex, Home/End jump to the ends, Down opens the focused menu, and once any menu is open, hovering or arrowing to an adjacent top menu switches to it. Each menu is a full Menu with items, checkbox and radio items, grouped labels, destructive items, shortcuts via Kbd, and nested submenus, sharing the Dropdown Menu's popup styling. The desktop-application command pattern, not website navigation.
useWhen: A desktop-application-style command bar for an app-like tool (a document editor, a data-grid console, an admin workbench)
Grouping many commands under persistent top menus (File / Edit / View / Help) that stay visible in the chrome
Commands that benefit from keyboard shortcuts, checkbox/radio state, and nested submenus organized by top menu
A workbench where users expect Alt-style menu traversal and roving between menus
avoidWhen: Primary navigation between the pages of an ordinary website (use the Navigation Menu, a nav landmark of links — a menubar is for app commands, not page links)
A single trigger that reveals one list of actions (use the Dropdown Menu)
A right-click / long-press action list on a region (use the Context Menu)
Marketing or content sites where a menubar's app affordances and roving keyboard model would confuse visitors

keyboard: The bar exposes role=menubar with aria-orientation; each top menu is a role=menuitem button with aria-haspopup=menu and aria-expanded
Left Arrow and Right Arrow rove between the top menus (roving tabindex — only one top menu is a tab stop); the row loops at the ends by default
Home jumps to the first top menu; End jumps to the last top menu
Down Arrow (or Enter, Space) opens the focused top menu and moves focus to its first item; Up Arrow opens onto the last item
Once any menu is open, arrowing Left/Right to an adjacent top menu (or hovering it) switches menus without closing the bar
Inside an open menu: Up/Down rove between items, Home/End jump to the ends, first-letter typeahead moves the highlight, Enter or Space activates (actions close; checkbox and radio items stay open)
Escape closes the open menu and returns focus to its top menu
Right Arrow opens a submenu and focuses its first item; Left Arrow closes it back to the submenu trigger (mirrored in RTL)
Disabled top menus and items stay reachable by the arrow keys (the APG focusable-disabled pattern) but carry aria-disabled and cannot be activated
Checkbox items expose aria-checked (menuitemcheckbox) and radio items expose aria-checked (menuitemradio)
accessibility.targetSize: 44px project default (top menus and items are min-h-11)
notes: The open/highlighted top menu and highlighted items pair their muted fill with a font-weight bump, so the active element is never signalled by color alone (WCAG 1.4.1); the roving focus ring and aria-expanded carry state in forced-colors mode.
The bar and every popup keep visible borders so forced-colors mode paints a boundary; popup motion is gated behind motion-safe.
MenubarShortcut renders decorative (aria-hidden) Kbd key caps so an item's accessible name stays the command label alone; add aria-keyshortcuts to the MenubarItem to also announce the shortcut.
This is the desktop-application command pattern; for site navigation use a nav landmark of links, not a menubar.
Wrapped in AmbientDirection so Base UI reads the DOM dir from a provider: logical sides flip, Left/Right rove the correct way, and submenu chevrons mirror in RTL.
